intro: Erythranthe exigua, also known as dwarf monkey flower, is a species of flowering plant in the snapdragon family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erythranthe exigua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Erythranthe exigua is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin care.
